summary: Lowest-ranked side in the group (85th) but arriving in good heart under Darren Bazeley — impressive recent friendlies (4-1 v Chile, 1-0 v Ivory Coast, 1-1 v Norway, and competitive one-goal losses to Ecuador, Colombia and Poland). A 'courageous, possession-based' side in the OFC, leaning on midfield turnovers, a cross-heavy attack to Chris Wood, and set pieces.
style: Cross-heavy attack built around Chris Wood in the box — midfielders drift wide to overload a flank, then find a run in behind to cross (e.g. De Vries' assist vs Australia). Forces turnovers in midfield through ball-winners like Stamenic, and threatens consistently from set pieces.

counterReliance.note: Possession-and-crossing for Wood; wins it back via midfield turnovers

strengths: Cross supply to a genuine box poacher in Chris Wood
Strong set-piece threat — 3 of 4 goals vs Chile from corners; Stamenic scored from a corner vs Ukraine; Surman a free kick vs Norway
Thorough preparation and confidence from competitive friendlies
weaknesses: Step up in level from a weak OFC — most matches there are vs sides ranked 150th+
Reliant on Wood and set pieces for goals

keyPlayers.name: Chris Wood
keyPlayers.note: All-time leading scorer (45 in 88), elite poacher and penalty expert; one of two survivors from the 2010 World Cup squad

keyPlayers.name: Matthew Garbett
keyPlayers.note: Key to the wide overloads — drifts wide, makes underlapping runs, 3rd among League One CM/DMs for take-ons (2.6/90), and plays defence-splitting passes

keyPlayers.name: Marko Stamenić
keyPlayers.note: Covers ground to win the ball back across midfield; also a set-piece goal threat

tactics: Win it back in midfield, overload the flanks to cross for Wood, and lean on a strong set-piece routine. The level jump from OFC is the unknown, but the friendlies suggest they'll be competitive in a tight group.
notes: Previous World Cups (1982, 2010) both ended in the group stage; the 2010 side famously went unbeaten with three draws.
sources: The Athletic — 2026 World Cup tactical guide, Group G (5 Jun 2026)
John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
